name: SMART-1
description: ESA's first lunar mission, 2003-2006. Used solar-electric (ion) propulsion to spiral out from a geostationary transfer orbit to lunar capture — a 14-month, fuel-efficient transit that pioneered low-thrust trajectories for ESA's later interplanetary missions. Deliberately impacted the lunar surface in 2006.
scale_fact: Polar orbit at ~470 km altitude. First operational use of ion propulsion for a lunar mission — a template for ESA's later BepiColombo and Hera deep-space missions.
